slides.title: JavaScript with Superpowers 🦸‍♂️
slides.content:
TypeScript (TS) is an open-source language developed by Microsoft. It's often described as a **superset** of JavaScript.

But what does that mean?
It means that **every valid JavaScript file is also a valid TypeScript file!** TypeScript simply adds new features on top of plain JavaScript.

slides.title: The Missing Feature 🔍
slides.content:
JavaScript is dynamically typed, meaning a variable can hold a number, and later be changed to hold a string without any complaints.

TypeScript introduces **Static Typing** to JavaScript. This means you specify what *type* of data a variable holds when you create it, and it can never hold anything else.

quizData.question: Which of the following best describes TypeScript?

options.text: A completely new language unrelated to JavaScript.
A superset of JavaScript that adds static typing.
A styling framework for web pages.

quizData.explanation: TypeScript builds on top of JavaScript by adding optional static typing and other features.

slides.title: The Analogy 🚗
slides.content:
Think of JavaScript as driving a car without a seatbelt. It's fast and flexible, but if you make a mistake, it hurts.

TypeScript is like driving the exact same car, but with a highly advanced seatbelt, airbags, and collision warning system. It restricts you slightly to keep you incredibly safe!

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Because TypeScript is a strict superset, you can migrate an existing JavaScript project to TypeScript simply by changing the file extensions from `.js` to `.ts`, and addressing any errors that pop up.
